claim: Using 10 parallel Claude Code agents instead of optimizing for 2x output is superior because it forces architectural thinking about system sustainability and self-sufficiency rather than incremental improvement
actionable: Design your AI coding workflow to support multiple parallel agents with independent worktrees, isolated databases, and separate contexts rather than trying to squeeze more from a single agent
source_title: Parallel Claude Code + Git Worktrees: This Setup Will Change How You Ship

claim: GitHub issues should be the specification input for implementation, and pull requests should be the input for validation—this creates a clean separation of concerns for parallel agentic development
actionable: Structure your work as: create GitHub issues with detailed specs, point each agent to an issue, output a PR from implementation, then validate via PR review in a fresh context
source_title: Parallel Claude Code + Git Worktrees: This Setup Will Change How You Ship

claim: Git worktrees enable true parallel development by giving each coding agent its own isolated copy of the codebase, preventing agents from overriding each other's changes
actionable: Use 'claude --worktree issue-<number>' to spin up isolated worktrees for each parallel agent session; this creates separate code environments that don't conflict
source_title: Parallel Claude Code + Git Worktrees: This Setup Will Change How You Ship

claim: A coding agent should never validate its own work in the same context window—fresh context reviews catch bugs and bias that the implementing agent missed due to conversational momentum
actionable: Run '/clear' to wipe context, then use 'review PR' in a separate Claude Code session to provide unbiased code review based on the pull request diff alone
source_title: Parallel Claude Code + Git Worktrees: This Setup Will Change How You Ship

claim: Database branching (e.g., via Neon) is critical for parallel agentic development—each worktree needs isolated data to test migrations and inserts without conflicts or corrupting production
actionable: Set up database branching for each worktree using Neon or similar tools; each agent gets a database branch that inherits production schema and data but is isolated for testing
source_title: Parallel Claude Code + Git Worktrees: This Setup Will Change How You Ship

claim: Port conflicts occur when starting multiple instances of the same application—assign unique ports dynamically based on worktree name to allow parallel app instances to coexist
actionable: Create a startup script that assigns ports based on worktree name (e.g., base port 4000 + hash of worktree name) so agents can validate their changes without port collisions
source_title: Parallel Claude Code + Git Worktrees: This Setup Will Change How You Ship

claim: The self-healing layer evolves system reliability by fixing not just individual bugs, but the underlying rules, skills, and workflows that allowed those bugs to occur
actionable: After identifying bugs in PRs, ask the agent: 'What could we fix in our rules, skills, workflows, or claude.md so this bug doesn't happen again?' Then update your AI layer accordingly
source_title: Parallel Claude Code + Git Worktrees: This Setup Will Change How You Ship

claim: Using Sonnet or Haiku for specialized sub-agents (like codebase analysis or web research) and Opus for complex implementation significantly reduces token costs while maintaining reliability
actionable: Use '/model' to switch models per task; reserve Opus for reasoning-heavy implementation, use Sonnet for code review, and Haiku for searches and simple analysis
source_title: Parallel Claude Code + Git Worktrees: This Setup Will Change How You Ship

claim: Installing dependencies upfront for each worktree rather than during implementation keeps agents focused and reduces validation cycle time
actionable: Run 'npm install' (or equivalent) as part of worktree setup script before invoking the coding agent, so it can focus on implementation and validation without dependency management overhead
source_title: Parallel Claude Code + Git Worktrees: This Setup Will Change How You Ship
